_meta.description: Multi-document scenarios that test the supersession + subtype + retrieval behavior the vocab-expansion spec targets. Each scenario specifies an ingest sequence (ordered), the expected final graph state assertions, and a set of retrieval queries with expected-top results.

assertion_format.final_state.live_relations: list of edges that MUST exist with valid_until IS NULL after the full ingest sequence
assertion_format.final_state.superseded_relations: list of edges that MUST exist with valid_until IS NOT NULL
assertion_format.final_state.absent_relations: list of (subject, type, object) triples that MUST NOT exist in any form
assertion_format.retrieval_queries: natural-language queries, with expected entity names in top-k results

scenarios.description: Alice's title is promoted at Atlas, then she changes employers to Beacon. Tests object-keyed HAS_TITLE supersession at Atlas plus subject-keyed WORKS_FOR supersession across employers.

superseded_relations.subject: Alice Chen
superseded_relations.notes: Superseded by doc_02 (same object, different subtype).

superseded_relations.subject: Alice Chen
superseded_relations.notes: Lives briefly after doc_02 then is supplanted when Alice leaves — though per spec, leaving Atlas does NOT cascade-supersede the title edge. Staff_engineer@Atlas remains live but stale. This is an explicit known limitation: cross-edge consistency is deferred.

retrieval_queries.query: Where does Alice Chen work now?

retrieval_queries.notes: Should surface the principal_engineer title at Beacon, not Atlas.

retrieval_queries.query: Who at Atlas does Alice still know?

scenarios.description: Alice moves cities (subject-keyed LIVES_IN supersession) and changes diet (subtype-keyed HAS_PREFERENCE supersession on dietary axis).

live_relations.subject: Alice Chen
live_relations.notes: Favorite-coffee axis is different from the dietary axis, so this survives.

superseded_relations.subject: Alice Chen
superseded_relations.notes: If the extractor emits this from doc_07's 'used to be vegetarian' phrasing.

retrieval_queries.query: Does Alice still live in Seattle?
retrieval_queries.notes: Temporal filter should prevent Seattle from dominating the answer.

scenarios.description: Multi-hop over family + preferences. Tests that FAMILY_OF remains additive (Alice has multiple family members) and that hop-2 queries can reach a family member's preference.

retrieval_queries.query: What does Alice's daughter like to do?

retrieval_queries.notes: 2-hop: Alice → FAMILY_OF → Daniel → WORKS_FOR → Rainfall Records.

scenarios.description: Events anchored to DateTime entities with HAPPENED_ON. Distinguishes past occurrence (subtype=null) from scheduled (subtype=scheduled).

retrieval_queries.query: What conference did Alice attend in June?

scenarios.description: Tests subtype-keyed supersession on HAS_PREFERENCE — each preference axis (favorite_cuisine, favorite_programming_language, etc.) has exactly one live value per subject.

live_relations.subject: Alice Chen
live_relations.object: The Soul of a New Machine

final_state.notes: All preferences coexist because each has a distinct subtype (different axis). If a doc ingested 'Alice's favorite programming language is now Rust', THAT would supersede the Python edge via subtype-keyed functional.

retrieval_queries.query: What is Alice's favorite programming language?

expect_top_names: The Soul of a New Machine

scenarios.description: Ingest all 20 docs in order. Tests the system end-to-end: multi-hop traversal across scenarios, reinforcement, and retrieval quality under the full graph.

final_state.notes: Exact counts depend on entity resolution; minima ensure no catastrophic loss.

retrieval_queries.query: Who did Alice recommend for a job?
Does Alice's daughter go to the same school where her uncle teaches?
retrieval_queries.notes: 3-hop: Alice → FAMILY_OF → Clara → MEMBER_OF → Maplewood; Maplewood ← LEADS ← Daniel ← FAMILY_OF ← Alice (Daniel is her brother, so Clara's uncle).

retrieval_queries.query: What did Alice present at the conference?
Who does Alice's husband work for?
What is Alice's relationship with Hiro Tanaka?
retrieval_queries.notes: Tests that historical REPORTS_TO and LEADS edges remain queryable even after supersession.

scenarios.description: Tests that Phase 3 cross-session filtering (#20) composes correctly with the expanded vocab. Ingest the same Alice docs under two session_ids; verify session filter isolates.

retrieval_queries.notes: Session A scope — tech prefs are visible, Ben's job details are not.
Empty — Ben's WORKS_FOR edge was ingested under session-B only.
